title: The Brethren.
content:
Slumber and Sleep, two brethren ordain'd by the gods to their service,

    Were by Prometheus implored, comfort to give to his race;
    But though so light to the gods, too heavy for man was their burden,

    We in their slumber find sleep, we in their sleep meet with death.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Johann Wolfgang von Goethe, titled "The Brethren.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
